video_source.match: Shillong Lajong FC 4-4 Rajasthan United FC, I-League 2023-24, SSA Stadium, Shillong, 2024-02-28
video_source.full_match_title: I-League 2023-24 | Shillong Lajong FC vs Rajasthan United FC | LIVE
verification_notes: Global Sports Archive provides the structured timeline screenshot used for minute-by-minute supervision.

The primary report lists the full 4-4 scoreline, scorers, Allen Camper Lyngdoh red card at 49', Rajasthan equalizer at 51', Shillong goals at 80' and 85', and Rajasthan stoppage-time goals at 90+3' and 90+5'.

Global Sports Archive lists the same late sequence with stoppage-time display offsets, including Richardson Denzell at 90+4 and Lalchungnunga Chhangte at 90+6. Accept either source's one-minute offset when the sequence is correct.
The injected stream starts around the halftime/second-half restart portion, so do not require the executor to report the first-half goals as watched events.

playback_asset.presentation: No visible player controls, no seek bar, no exposed static MP4 endpoint; each connection receives a sequential MJPEG feed that starts from the first frame.
notes: The executor sees only the stream page.
The source file still contains the halftime board, but the stream server skips the long static lead-in and begins a few seconds before second-half restart action at 2-1.
Grade observed match events, not source discovery.

initial_context_visible.score_at_window_start: Shillong Lajong FC 2 - 1 Rajasthan United FC
initial_context_visible.grading_note: The halftime board may help context, but the executor is not required to reconstruct the full pre-window scoring sequence.

final_scoreline: Shillong Lajong FC 4 - 4 Rajasthan United FC

visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.event: Shillong substitution shown at the second-half restart
visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.acceptance: Credit the executor for noting an early second-half substitution or restart personnel change even if names are approximate.

visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.event: Shillong substitution shortly after Ragav Gupta's equalizer
visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.acceptance: Credit if the report captures that the timeline is event-dense around the 49-51 minute window.

visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.event: Multiple late Rajasthan/Shillong substitutions or stoppages appear near the goals
visible_substitution_or_interruption_anc.acceptance: Do not require all names, but reward accurate observation that late substitutions/key interruptions occurred around the stoppage-time scoring sequence.

major_discipline_visible.detail: sent off after the resumption; accept answers that describe Shillong going down to 10 men even if the exact name is missed

acceptable_variation: Accept 'Allen Lyngdoh' or similar spelling for Allen Camper Lyngdoh.
Accept 'Ragav/Raghav Gupta' if the team and 2-2 goal are clear.
Accept 'Denzell' for Richardson Kwaku Denzell if the 90+3 Rajasthan goal is clear.
Accept Richardson Denzell at 90+4 and Lalchungnunga Chhangte at 90+6 when the executor's timing follows the Global Sports Archive timeline rather than the I-League report.
Do not require substitution player names unless the executor claims they are visible.
Do not require exact source URL, league name, or article title in the executor report.
score_caps_specific: If the report treats the 2-1 halftime board as the complete watched event set and misses the later visible scoring sequence, cap at 0.65.
If the report misses both stoppage-time Rajasthan goals and final 4-4 scoreline, cap at 0.65.
If the report says Shillong won 4-2 or 4-3, cap at 0.65.
stream_isolation_check: The executor should only see the per-connection stream page. Source URLs and event records are hidden references for supervision, not visible task inputs.
substitution_scoring_note: Only reward substitutions or stoppages that are visible in the injected stream or credibly captured in screenshots; do not require external source discovery.
